Key Takeaways:
o It’s time to go deeper on lists, response rates, and lists in general. When I was
getting started I learned a lot about direct marketing and direct mail. In general,
the typical response rate is 1-2% in direct mail
o People always wonder what the typical response rate is for a launch. The thing is it
completely depends on your niche, list, offer, copy, etc. there’s absolutely nothing
typical. Generally something better than 1-2% is what you should shoot for
o The best conversion comes from having a warm list of people that have been
getting valuable content from you, and now you give them a product that they’ve
been wanting. In PLF launches we’ve seen anywhere from $50-100 per
name/subscriber. Generally the average is $75-85 per name
o Measuring this is difficult because these launches usually have traffic coming from
JV’s, and they’re entering the sequence of PLC at different points. Note: this
number comes from the launch list not the main prospect list
o The 3 biggest factors are your offer, copy, and list. With a launch list you
get a great list so that’s taken care of. The better your conversion is the better you
do overall.
o Example: if you know every name on your launch list is worth $100, then
you can tell your JV partners that on average each person they send that
joins your list will earn them $50 (50% of commission).
o If you’re looking for partners or spending on advertising, the better your
conversions are the easier it will be. That’s why the sideways sales letter is so
important - when they go through the sequence they are incredibly value
o This can be advanced, but I wanted to expose you to it because getting the math
right gives you huge power

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Let's just go a little bit deeper on the launch list and list in general, and response
rates and that kind of thing.
When I first started, I was very lucky that some of the earliest teaching that I was
exposed to was about direct marketing and direct mail. Frankly, that's the only thing that
was available back then because no one was teaching anything about marketing online or on
the internet because there really wasn't any. No one had a clue. There was just no knowledge
base, so I studied a lot of direct marketing stuff.
Typical response rate, what I learned back then, typical response rate might be 1 to
2% if you do some type of direct mail. That's pretty funny in itself because the difference
between 1% and 2% is just, it's huge, it's a double, right? I mean, if you send out a thousand
pieces and you get a 1% response, you're going to sell 10. If you got a 2% response, you're
going to sell 20. You double your business going from 1 to 2%, so it's pretty crazy. It's a
pretty big range.
But in any case, people wonder what's a typical response in launches? I guess it’s sort
of like that 1 to 2%. It's all over the place. It's impossible to answer. It completely depends on
your market and your price and your position in the market, and your list, and your offer,
and how you well you make that offer, your copy. There's absolutely nothing typical, but
generally I'm looking for better than 1 to 2%.
In niches, you can, especially if you have a small list and you have a warm
relationship, you can have way better than 1 to 2%. To show you the upper end, I actually did
a launch once where I had a tiny, slivered niche. It was a great, big niche but it was just like a
tiny, little sliver and I just had this whole list universe of 300 people. I had a very warm
relationship with those 300 people. It was sort of like the perfect storm in terms of
conversion. So, these 300 people and I had a very warm relationship.
I was trying to make the most difficult sale ever which was, I had been giving them
this service for free and now I wanted to charge them for it. There's no harder sale to make
than to say, "Hey, I know I've been giving this for free for like 3 years from me, but now I
want to charge you for it." Toughest sale in the world, but besides that, everything else is
perfect. It was a community. They were tied in the community. There were very much tied
into that. They loved me. I've been supporting them. I had this warm relationship I could
target. Since there's only 300 of them and I knew them, knew my avatar really well, I could
target the message to them really perfectly.
In any case, my response rate was 99%. I got 297 out of 300 to say, " Yes, I'll buy it
from you." I don't think I'm ever going to improve on a 99% response rate. Like I said, it's
not typical like a perfect storm. If I went out to the broader niche. There's no way I would
have had any kind of conversion at all, but it was absolutely a perfect fit. It was essentially
like a new list that was carefully curetted. Again, there is absolutely nothing typical but the
way we will see the best conversion is if you carefully built a list over some period of time
and have a really warm relationship and they love you, and they're just waiting to buy from
you, and now you put together a launch that's perfectly fit for them.
